narration: Define a function named bubbleSort that takes an array of integers as a parameter.
Initialize an integer variable i to 0 and set up a for loop that iterates while i is less than the length of the array minus one.
Use a temporary variable to store the value of the current element at index i, then assign the value from index i+1 to index i.
Enclose the sorting loop in a while loop that continues as long as a boolean flag named swapped is true.
Initialize the boolean variable swapped to true before entering the outer while loop.
Reset the swapped variable to false before each iteration of the inner for loop.
Set the swapped variable to true if any swap occurs during the inner loop iteration.
Execute the bubble sort algorithm on an array of 10 random integers and verify the output is sorted.
Execute the bubble sort algorithm on an array of 1,000 random integers and verify the output is sorted.
Execute the execution of the bubble sort algorithm on an array of 1,000,000 random integers and verify the output is sorted.
